revision.text:
[[fa:فستیوال آکوروشی]][[ja:赤穂義士祭]] {{Event information |イベント名称=Ako Gishi Festival |英文表記=Ako Gishi Festival |種類= |image=Ako Gishisai De09 13.jpg |imageサイズ=250px |image説明=Gishi Procession at the Ako Gishi Festival |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=Every year on {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|qid=Q2382|lt=December 14|1=December 14}} |初回開催={{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36) |会場=Ikitsugi Square and around Oshiro Street |主催=Ako Gishi Festival Support Association |共催=Local companies and shops |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |特別協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数=Approx. 90,000 (organizer announcement)
As of 2014Asahi Shimbun [http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGDG3Q16GDGPIHB004.html "Ken Matsudaira as Oishi Kuranosuke, 90,000 attend Ako Gishi Festival"] December 14, 2014 |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅={{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Akō Line|en|Akō Line|qid=Q1049803|lt=Akō Line|1=Akō Line}} {{ill|Banshū-Akō Station|en|Banshū-Akō Station|qid=Q4857136|lt=Banshū-Akō Station|1=Banshū-Akō Station}} |直通バス=None |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} Ako Gishi Festival (赤穂義士祭, Akō Gishi Sai) is a festival held annually on {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|qid=Q2382|lt=December 14|1=December 14}} in {{ill|Akō, Hyōgo|en|Akō, Hyōgo|qid=Q424813|lt=Akō, Hyōgo|1=Akō, Hyōgo}}, commemorating the {{ill|Forty-seven Ronin|ja|赤穂義士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Forty-seven Ronin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤穂事件の人物一覧|1=Forty-seven Ronin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3136847}} and their famous raid on this date.This date is based on the old calendar; in the current Gregorian calendar, the date would be {{ill|January 30|en|January 30|qid=Q2287|lt=January 30|1=January 30}}. It has been celebrated since {{ill|1903|en|1903|qid=Q2045|lt=1903|1=1903}} (Meiji 36), becoming the largest event in Akō City with over 100 occurrences. == Overview == The festival's main attraction is the "{{ill|Chūshingura|en|Chūshingura|qid=Q683379|lt=Chūshingura|1=Chūshingura}} Parade," featuring a brass band parade by local elementary school students, sword-fighting performances by {{ill|Toei Kenkai|ja|東映剣会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toei Kenkai|1=Toei Kenkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128868025}}, "Gishi Musume Jinriki Dochū" with girls dressed in elegant princess costumes, a "Daimyo procession" reminiscent of the {{ill|Sankin-kōtai|en|Sankin-kōtai|qid=Q1151844|lt=Sankin-kōtai|1=Sankin-kōtai}} of Akō Domain lords, "Gishi-den procession" featuring characters from kabuki, bunraku, and kōdan associated with Chūshingura, float performances reenacting seven famous scenes from Chūshingura, and the "Gishi procession" portraying the forty-seven ronin marching to Sengakuji in Takanawa, Edo, immersing thousands of spectators in the Edo-period atmosphere. Additional events include memorial services at {{ill|Kagaku-ji|ja|花岳寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagaku-ji|1=Kagaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3191843}} where the ronin are buried, rituals at {{ill|Ōishi Shrine|ja|大石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōishi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Oishi Shrine|qid=Q11438245}}, and a "Chūshingura Product Exchange Fair" organized by neighboring municipalities and friendship cities. The week leading up to the festival is "Chūshingura Week," featuring various daily events throughout the city. === Activities === * Venue - Ikitsugi Square, Oshiro Street vicinity * Organizer - Ako Gishi Festival Support Association ;Chūshingura Week :A weeklong celebration preceding the festival, showcasing nearly 100 groups and shops hosting special limited-time events throughout Akō City. ;Gishi Memorial Eve Festival :A lantern procession illuminated by traditional candles and bamboo lanterns around Ōishi Shrine and Kagaku-ji Temple, including hospitality with traditional food like kasu-jiru, ton-jiru, nira-zōsui, and toshi-iri soba. ;Ako Gishi Festival (Annual {{ill|December 14|en|December 14|qid=Q2382|lt=December 14|1=December 14}}) :Main event commemorating the raid, featuring the elaborate Gishi parade. ;Chūshingura Product Exchange Fair :Held at Akō Castle's Sannomaru and Samurai Residence Park, featuring booths from Chūshingura Summit cities offering local products and cultural performances. === Special Guests and Performances === Notable participants over the years have included stars such as {{ill|Hiroko Yakushimaru|en|Hiroko Yakushimaru|qid=Q1054683|lt=Hiroko Yakushimaru|1=Hiroko Yakushimaru}}, {{ill|Masahiko Tsugawa|en|Masahiko Tsugawa|qid=Q3098359|lt=Masahiko Tsugawa|1=Masahiko Tsugawa}}, {{ill|Ken Matsudaira|en|Ken Matsudaira|qid=Q1154195|lt=Ken Matsudaira|1=Ken Matsudaira}}, {{ill|Teruhiko Saigō|en|Teruhiko Saigō|qid=Q1092085|lt=Teruhiko Saigō|1=Teruhiko Saigō}}, {{ill|Nakamura Baijaku II|en|Nakamura Baijaku II|qid=Q3547153|lt=Baijaku Nakamura II|1=Nakamura Baijaku II}}, {{ill|Hideki Takahashi|en|Hideki Takahashi|qid=Q3545942|lt=Hideki Takahashi|1=Hideki Takahashi}}, and others. Special performances included artificial snow displays and projection mapping. == Access == * About 7 minutes' walk south from JR {{ill|Banshū-Akō Station|en|Banshū-Akō Station|qid=Q4857136|lt=Banshū-Akō Station|1=Banshū-Akō Station}} on the {{ill|Akō Line|en|Akō Line|qid=Q1049803|lt=Akō Line|1=Akō Line}} == Gishi Festivals Nationwide == Similar Gishi festivals are held throughout Japan, notably at Sengakuji Temple in Tokyo, in Hokkaido's Sunagawa City, and in Kasama City, Ibaraki Prefecture. == See Also == * {{ill|Forty-seven rōnin|en|Forty-seven rōnin|qid=Q822472|lt=Akō incident|1=Forty-seven rōnin}} * {{ill|Chūshingura|en|Chūshingura|qid=Q683379|lt=Chūshingura|1=Chūshingura}} * {{ill|Ōishi Shrine|ja|大石神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōishi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438245|1=Oishi Shrine}} * {{ill|Ako City Museum of History|ja|赤穂市立歴史博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akō City Museum of History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635880|1=Ako City Museum of History}} == References ==
== External Links == *[https://ako-kankou.jp/ Akō Tourism Association] *[https://all-ako.com/ Chūshingura Week] {{Pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|赤穂義士祭|version=103397158|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:赤穂義士祭]] {{Commons category|Akō Gishisai}} [[fa:فستیوال آکوروشی]] {{wikidata link|Q11635899}} [[Category:兵庫県の祭り]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Akō, Hyōgo]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:Winter events in the Kinki region]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
